name: Zolai DPO Builder
role: DPO Preference Pair Generation for ZVS Dialect Alignment
goal: Generate DPO preference pairs that teach the model to prefer correct Tedim ZVS over wrong dialect forms
rules: rejected = same output with wrong dialect substitutions
Never fabricate — only substitute known ZVS violations
Each pair must have a meaningful difference — not just one word swap
Validate chosen output passes ZVS rules before writing
Target: 10,000-60,000 pairs minimum (Basque team used 61k, got +7pts)
Also generate pairs from: grammar errors (lo leh → kei a leh), plural errors (uh+i), SOV violations

pronoun_system.note: Verified from all 3 Bible versions (TDB77, TBR17, Tedim2010)

i.function: 1st person plural subject prefix (we) — used with verb directly
i.corpus: Ka itte aw, tu-in Pasian' tate i hi hi (TDB77 1Jn 3:2)

examples: Pasian in eite hong it = God loves us
eite.corpus: Pasian in eite hong it a, eite mawhna hong lak ding Tapa hong khal hi (TDB77 1Jn 4:10)

kei te.function: NOT standard 'we' — corpus shows 0 occurrences as subject pronoun in Bible
kei te.note: kei = I/me (1st person singular). 'kei te' appears only in specific emphatic contexts like 'kei teek' (just me/only me). Do NOT use as standard 'we'.
kei te.corpus_evidence: kei te appears in: 'kei teek ka hi' (it is only me) — NOT as plural we

word_notes.love (verb): it — corpus: 'Pasian in eite hong it' (God loves us), 'eite khat leh khat ki-it' (love one another)
word_notes.love (noun): itna — corpus: 'Pasian' itna' (love of God), 'itna in Pasian tawh kipawl' (love is of God)
word_notes.lungdam: glad / thankful / happy — NOT love. Do NOT use for 'love'
word_notes.us (object): eite — corpus: 'Pasian in eite hong it' (God loves us)
word_notes.we (subject): i (prefix) or eite (emphatic) — NEVER kei te
word_notes.go: pai — directional: 'va pai' (go away), 'hong pai' (come toward speaker)
word_notes.if you don't go (away): Na pai kei a leh / Na pai kei leh
word_notes.if you don't come (toward): Nong pai kei a leh / Nong pai kei leh
word_notes.hong: directional particle — toward speaker. 'Pasian in eite hong it' = God loves us (coming to us)

pair_types.note: it = love (verb). eite = us. hong = toward speaker (directional). Verified from 1Jn 4:10 TDB77.

example.chosen: Pasian in eite hong it hi.
example.rejected: Pathian in eite hong it hi.

pair_types.note: Na = you going away from speaker. Nong = you coming toward speaker.
i = we (subject prefix). 'I pai hi' = We went. Corpus: 'i pai uh' is wrong — never add uh with i.
kei te is NOT 'we'. eite or i is correct.

example.chosen: Pasian in eite hong it hi.
example.rejected: Pasian in kei te hong it hi.

alignment_methods.recommended: ORPO (arXiv:2403.07691) — combines SFT+alignment in one pass, saves compute
alignment_methods.alternative: DPO (trl.DPOTrainer) — separate alignment stage after SFT
alignment_methods.avoid: GRPO — overkill for current stage, needs reward function
alignment_methods.orpo_advantage: No need for separate SFT run; trains preference alignment simultaneously

evidence.basque: Corral et al. 2024: DPO on 61k translated UltraFeedback pairs → +7pts correct answers
evidence.urdu: arXiv:2502.16961: LoRA SFT on 41K instructions + 50K translation pairs → strong results
evidence.quality_vs_quantity: arXiv:2408.12780: diversity of SFT data > raw quantity for low-resource
evidence.warning: DPO/ORPO only works on language-adapted base model — run CPT first
